title: Fundraiser launched for family of Southborough crash victim, beloved local bartender
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A GoFundMe campaign supports the family of Crystal Johnson-Clementi, a local bartender who died after a crash in Southborough.
Johnson-Clementi, 37, survived breast cancer and leaves behind her husband, son, and several siblings, nieces, and nephews.
The fundraiser has raised over $46,000, nearing its goal, to assist her family with expenses after her passing.

title: Jayson Tatum: Active for the game, never questionable in the tunnel
author: LaDonna LaGuerre
bullets: Jayson Tatum returns to the Boston Celtics lineup after a 10-month absence, set to play at TD Garden.
Tatum is known for his pre-game fashion, with fans eagerly anticipating his outfit choices for the game.
Boston Celtics players like Neemias Queta and Jordan Walsh have also showcased distinctive fashion styles this season.

title: How Susan Hurley’s legacy inspired her son to run the Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Max McGillivray is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of his mother, Susan Hurley, who passed away in 2025.
Susan Hurley founded CharityTeams, raising $42 million for nonprofits and inspiring numerous charity runners at the Boston Marathon.
McGillivray supports Justice Health Initiative, continuing his mother's work, by running for essential healthcare for children in need.

title: Back Bay’s ‘ugly duckling’ is getting a makeover
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Developers Lourenco Carminati and Eagle Hill Capital bought the Back Bay property in 2024 for $7.85 million.
The 11-unit building at 18 Commonwealth Ave. is being gutted and rebuilt as an 8,100-square-foot luxury single-family home.
The renovated home will feature modern amenities, including a two-car garage, rooftop terrace, and elevator-served floors.

title: Country star Bailey Zimmerman surprises Boston bar crowd, picks up everyone’s tab
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Bailey Zimmerman surprised fans by paying the tab at J.J. Foley’s Cafe in Boston's South End.
Zimmerman visited the pub after performing at TD Garden during his 'Different Night Same Rodeo Tour.'
The visit coincides with J.J. Foley’s Cafe launching their St. Patrick's season celebrations and 117th birthday party.

title: Haitian man living in Boston dies in ICE custody, heightening concerns about detention centers
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Emmanuel Damas, a 56-year-old Haitian man, died in ICE custody in Arizona due to inadequate medical treatment for a toothache.
Lawmakers criticized ICE's care practices, citing Damas's untreated infection leading to sepsis and eventual hospitalization on life support.
Advocates and lawmakers demand accountability, highlighting recurring medical neglect at ICE detention centers run by for-profit CoreCivic.

title: Celtics officially list Jayson Tatum as ‘Available’ for game vs. Mavericks
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Jayson Tatum is officially listed as 'available' for the Celtics' game against the Mavericks after recovering from an Achilles injury.
The Celtics are 41-21 and second in the Eastern Conference despite Tatum's prolonged absence from the lineup.
Coach Joe Mazzulla will address Tatum's minutes and role as he returns to Boston's active roster.

title: Patriots reportedly agree to two-year deal with backup QB Tommy DeVito
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The New England Patriots signed backup QB Tommy DeVito to a two-year, $7.4 million deal with $2 million guaranteed.
DeVito, a New Jersey native, joined the Patriots in 2025 after being claimed off waivers from the Giants.
The Patriots' quarterback depth now includes Drake Maye as starter and backup QBs Tommy DeVito and Josh Dobbs.

title: South Shore mother and son stuck in Qatar amid war in Iran
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Stacey Schuhwerk and her son from Hingham are stuck in Qatar after U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran disrupted air travel.
U.S. Rep. Stephen Lynch is assisting constituents, including Schuhwerk, in finding ways to evacuate the Middle East safely.
The Trump administration criticized for insufficient evacuation efforts; tensions rise with potential troop deployment to Iran.

title: Tufts announces new proposed aquatics center for swimming and diving teams
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Tufts University plans to build a new aquatics center, replacing the 80-year-old Hamilton Pool.
The facility will feature a 50-by-25-meter pool, diving boards, and space for competitions and events.
Construction is expected to be completed by the start of the fall 2028 semester, pending city approval.
